title: This year’s theme for the International Day of Yoga is “Yoga for Healthy Ageing,” highlighting its role in promoting physical health, mental well-being, emotional resilience and active ageing
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
Key Updates
  • A countdown to International Day of Yoga
  • How PM Modi celebrated International Yoga Day since 2015
  • June 21, 2026 09:24
    President Murmu leads Yoga Day celebrations in Madhya Pradesh

    President Droupadi Murmu led the International Day of Yoga celebrations in Madhya Pradesh on Sunday (June 21, 2026), performing asanas at the state-level event in Jabalpur.

    Across the State, thousands of people participated in mass yoga sessions organised to mark the occasion.

    Ms. Murmu, accompanied by MP Governor Mangubhai Patel and Chief Minister Mohan Yadav, rolled out her mat to join the yoga session at the Garrison Ground in Jabalpur.

    In Bhopal, a large number of people assembled at the TT Nagar Stadium to practise yoga and spread the message of healthy living. The administration made elaborate arrangements for security, drinking water, medical aid and other basic facilities.

    In Indore, thousands of participants performed Bhramari Pranayama continuously for more than three minutes This reflected growing awareness about yoga and collective participation in wellness activities, organisers said. -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 09:23
    T.N. Governor participates in 12th International Yoga Day

    Tamil Nadu Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar on Sunday (June 21, 2026) participated in the 12th International Yoga Day celebrations organised at Tamil Nadu Agricultural University in Coimbatore.

    The event highlights the importance of yoga for a healthy lifestyle and holistic well-being, aligning with this year’s theme of enhancing healthspan alongside lifespan.

    The celebration, beginning with the Vande Mataram song, has brought together students, faculty, and the public to embrace yoga as a way of life for harmony, balance, and inner peace.

    Union Minister of State for Information and Broadcasting L Murugan also performed Yoga at Coimbatore. Union Minister of State for Defence Sanjay Seth performed Yoga at Rameswaram. Central Government departments in the State also have organised Yoga events in various places. -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 08:59
    Yoga celebrations in Vijayawada

    VIJAYAWADA, Andhra Pradesh, June 21, 2026: Schoolchildren participate in a mass yoga session during the International Day of Yoga celebrations at the Indira Gandhi Municipal Stadium in Vijayawada on Sunday. Hundreds of students, government employees, yoga practitioners and members of the public joined the event, which received an enthusiastic response across the city. PHOTO: G.N. RAO / THE HINDU

  • June 21, 2026 08:52
    Yoga guides people in achieving holistic health: J&K LG Sinha

    Jammu and Kashmir Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha on Sunday (June 21, 2026) led the yoga day celebrations here and noted that it was helpful in maintaining both physical and mental health.

    Addressing the gathering at SKICC on the banks of Dal Lake here, he said it guides us toward a more holistic realm of health and happiness.

    “This year’s Yoga Day theme is ‘Yoga for Healthy Ageing’. With rising stress and modern health challenges, Yoga offers a powerful ray of hope. Let’s take a step toward wellness today by participating and committing to a healthier lifestyle,” Mr. Sinha said.

    He urged the people of Jammu Kashmir to actively participate in yoga events and champion the movement toward a healthier lifestyle.

    “Yoga stands as an invaluable treasure of our ancient Indian heritage, which harmonises mind and body, bridges the bond between humanity and nature, and guides us toward a more holistic realm of health and happiness,” he added. -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 08:32
    Practice yoga to keep diseases at bay: Adityanath

    Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath on Sunday (June 21, 2026) stressed on the importance of yoga, saying it helps keep one free from disease.

    “During childhood we used to hear that a healthy mind resides in a healthy body. A healthy mind will have a healthy brain. If brain is healthy, then soul will also be healthy. And in that God will reside. The beginning (of this) is a healthy body,” he said, while urging people to take up yoga The chief minister along with Ayush Minister Dayashankar Mishra ‘Dayalu’ also did yoga in Jhansi. -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 08:12
    International Yoga Day - Chennai

    Yoga.jpg

  • June 21, 2026 08:10
    Military personnel join celebrations across India 

    From high-altitude mountains to parade grounds in the plains, a large number of armed forces personnel took part in yoga sessions across the country to mark International Day of Yoga on Sunday (June 21, 2026).

    All military commands of the three services — the Army, Navy and the Air Force — have been gearing up for this annual event, which seeks to promote physical fitness, mental resilience and holistic well-being.

    In the national capital, the Indian Army is hosting the event at the Cariappa Parade Ground at Delhi Cantonment.

    Army jawans and others, positioned in a huge grid formation, stretched their arms and legs to perform various ‘asanas’.

    “The yoga session at the Cariappa Parade Ground began at around 6 am. About 3,500 people gathered at the venue for the event, including Army personnel and their families, several defence attaches, NCC cadets, and school children,” a senior Army officer told PTI.

    Similar events have been lined up at various Army commands — Northern, Western, Eastern, Southern, Central and South Western Commands. -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 08:08
    Meghalaya: Make yoga part of daily life, Rajnath Singh urges people

    Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Sunday (June 21, 2026) said yoga has evolved into a global people’s movement under Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s leadership and called on citizens to embrace it as part of their everyday routine.

    Celebrating the International Day of Yoga at the Indian Air Force’s Advanced Landing Ground in Upper Shillong, Singh extended greetings to people and described yoga as “not merely the foundation of a healthy body, but a path to living a balanced and disciplined life.” “Through the continuous efforts and visionary leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, this cultural heritage of India has today become a global people’s movement,” the Defence Minister said in a post on X.

    “Yoga is a symbol of that power from India’s ancient tradition of knowledge, which is paving the way for the welfare of all humanity,” he said.

    Urging people to adopt the practice in their daily lives, Mr. Singh said, “Let us, on this yoga day, resolve to make it an integral part of our daily lives for building a healthy body, a peaceful mind, and a strong nation.” -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 08:00
    Yoga India’s timeless gift to world: Manipur CM

    Manipur Chief Minister Y Khemchand Singh on Sunday (June 21, 2026) described yoga as India’s timeless gift to the world and urged people to embrace the practice for a healthier, happier and more mindful society.

    Mr. Singh joined Governor Ajay Kumar Bhalla and hundreds of participants in observing the 12th International Day of Yoga at the Khuman Lampak Indoor Stadium here.

    The CM also watched the live telecast of Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s address from Kolkata’s Red Road.

    In a post on X, Mr. Singh said, “Joined people across Manipur in celebrating the 12th International Day of Yoga under the theme, ‘Yoga for Healthy Ageing.” He also said “Yoga, India’s timeless gift to the world, promotes physical well-being, mental resilience, and inner harmony” and called on all to “embrace this practice and work towards a healthier, happier, and more mindful society.” -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 07:32
    Yoga has become world’s largest community celebration, says PM Modi

    Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday (June 21, 2026) said yoga has become the world’s largest community celebration, bringing people together across countries and cultures.

    Speaking at the 12th International Day of Yoga celebrations in Kolkata, Mr. Modi said June 21, the longest day on the Earth, is now recognised worldwide as a day of yoga.

    Yoga has become world’s largest community celebration, says PM Modi

    PM Modi declares yoga as the world's largest community celebration, emphasizing its role in promoting unity and healthy ageing.

  • June 21, 2026 07:23
    International Yoga Day in Vijayawada
  • June 21, 2026 07:19
    Thousands join PM Modi’s yoga event in Kolkata

    Prime Minister Narendra Modi led the nation in observing the 12th International Day of Yoga from Kolkata’s Red Road, where thousands of people joined him in performing the morning exercises.

    Participating in the Common Yoga Protocol session, Mr. Modi performed yoga alongside thousands of practitioners at the iconic venue, flanked by the Indian Army’s Eastern Command headquarters on one side and the sprawling Maidan on the other. -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 07:12
    Yoga helps us live in balanced manner, says PM Modi

    Addressing the nation during participating in the 12th International Yoga Day, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said “Yoga helps us live in balanced manner, teaches us dos and don’ts.”

    The Prime Minister further said “Our target is to be more energetic at 50 than we were at 30.”

    He said “Yoga helps us find path to physical fitness through mental health.” 

    “We should not restrict yoga to only particular occasions, it must be made part of our lives,” said Mr. Modi. -- PTI

    ​

    ​

  • June 21, 2026 07:05
    Build a world where every generation can lead a healthy life: Guterres 

    United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres extended greetings on the occasion of the International Day of Yoga, highlighting yoga’s global reach and its role in promoting physical and mental well-being across generations.

    In a post on X, Mr. Guterres said yoga, which traces its origins to ancient India, has evolved into a universal practice embraced by people of all faiths and cultures.

    “From its deep roots in ancient India, yoga has become truly universal, helping millions of people of all faiths & cultures find calm, build strength & live with purpose,” Mr. Guterres said. -- ANI

  • June 21, 2026 07:01
    Yoga not restricted to any age group, it is expression of human spirit, says PM Modi

    Prime Minister Narendra Modi led the nation by participating at the 12th International Yoga Day from Red Road in Kolkata on Sunday (June 21, 2026). 

    Addressing the nation, Mr. Modi said “June 21 marks longest day on Earth, it has now become largest community celebration day because of yoga”.

    The Prime Minister said “Yoga brings people together; I congratulate people of the world on this occasion. Yoga is not just physical exercise, not restricted to any age group; it is expression of human spirit.” -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 06:57
    Delaware governor issues proclamation on International Day of Yoga

    Delaware Governor Matthew Meyer has issued a proclamation in recognition of the International Day of Yoga in the state and urged residents to explore the practice for a healthier lifestyle.

    In a proclamation, Meyer said yoga is a 5,000-year-old ancient practice from India that integrates the mind, body, and spirit to enhance overall health and well-being.

    He said the practice of yoga fosters physical strength, flexibility, mental clarity, and emotional balance, offering valuable tools for managing stress and improving community health.

    “Now, Therefore, I, Matthew Meyer, Governor, do hereby proclaim June 21, 2026, as INTERNATIONAL DAY OF YOGA in the State of Delaware, and encourage all residents to explore this practice for a healthier lifestyle,” the proclamation read. -- PTI

  • June 21, 2026 06:45
    A countdown to International Day of Yoga

    At a time when the world is navigating conflict, uncertainty, and rising psychological strain, the idea of peace demands renewed attention. Traditionally approached through diplomacy and policy, peace is often framed as an external goal, negotiated across borders and institutions. Yet, despite these efforts, societies remain increasingly unsettled, suggesting that peace cannot be secured externally alone. It must also be cultivated within. Yoga facilitates this journey.

    A countdown to International Day of Yoga

    The countdown to the International Day of Yoga, celebrating inner peace and global unity through mindfulness and emotional regulation.

  • June 21, 2026 06:45
    How PM Modi celebrated International Yoga Day since 2015

    Yoga has become a cultural link that unites individuals from different continents, with the yearly celebrations of International Yoga Day evolving into a worldwide phenomenon. Yoga comes from the Sanskrit word “yuj” and means to “join” or to “yoke”. It is a pathway to positive changes in body and mind.

    ​

    How PM Modi celebrated International Yoga Day since 2015

    Here’s how International Day of Yoga has evolved into a global celebration. See how India celebrated Yoga day from 2015 to 2025

    ​

  • June 21, 2026 06:44
    Main event to be held in Kolkata on June 21

    Prime Minister Narendra Modi will lead the main event of the International Day of Yoga in Kolkata on June 21, Union Minister for Ayush Prataprao Jadhav said.

    Addressing a press conference, Mr. Jadhav said this year’s theme, ‘Yoga for Healthy Ageing’, highlights the role of yoga in promoting physical health, mental well-being and healthy ageing.

    International Yoga Day 2026: Main event to be held in Kolkata on June 21; PM to lead

    Prime Minister Narendra Modi will lead the International Day of Yoga event in Kolkata on June 21, focusing on 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing.'

  • June 21, 2026 06:43
    PM Modi to lead nation from Kolkata

    Prime Minister Narendra Modi will on Sunday (June 21, 2026) morning lead the nation in observing the 12th International Day of Yoga from the Red Road in Kolkata.

    This year’s theme for the International Day of Yoga is “Yoga for Healthy Ageing,” highlighting its role in promoting physical health, mental well-being, emotional resilience and active ageing, contributing to an improved quality of life.

    International Yoga Day 2026: PM Modi to lead nation from Kolkata

    Prime Minister Modi leads International Yoga Day celebrations in Kolkata, promoting "Yoga for Healthy Ageing" with thousands of participants.

Published - June 21, 2026 06:56 am IST

title: Yoga has become world’s largest community celebration, says PM Modi
author: PTI
content_html:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday (June 21, 2026) said yoga has become the world's largest community celebration, bringing people together across countries and cultures.

Speaking at the 12th International Day of Yoga celebrations in Kolkata, Mr. Modi said June 21, the longest day on the Earth, is now recognised worldwide as a day of yoga.

International Yoga Day 2026 LIVE

"June 21, which marks the longest day on Earth, has now become the largest community celebration day because of yoga. Yoga brings people together. I congratulate the people of the world on this occasion," he said.

From physical wellness to inner peace, yoga enriches every aspect of life. Delighted to join this year's celebrations in Kolkata. https://t.co/75UZECw8JR

— Narendra Modi (@narendramodi) June 21, 2026

The Prime Minister also congratulated the people of Kolkata for pursuing the 'Swachhata Se Swagat' initiative with dedication.

Referring to this year's International Day of Yoga theme, 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing', Modi said the ancient practice plays a crucial role in promoting physical health, mental well-being and active ageing, thereby improving the quality of life.

"Yoga is not just physical exercise. It is not restricted to any age group. It is an expression of the human spirit," he said.

Emphasising the importance of healthy ageing, Mr. Modi said efforts must be made to ensure that advancing age does not reduce human potential.

"When we speak of yoga for healthy ageing, we can work to ensure that age does not reduce human potential," he said.

"Our target is to be more flexible at 40 than we were at 20. We aim to be more energetic at 50 than we were at 30," he added.

The Prime Minister also said people should not restrict yoga to only particular occasions, and it must be made part of people's lives.

Published - June 21, 2026 07:31 am IST

title: International Yoga Day 2026: PM Modi to lead nation from Kolkata
author: PTI
content_html:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi will on Sunday (June 21, 2026) morning lead the nation in observing the 12th International Day of Yoga from the Red Road here.

This year's theme for the International Day of Yoga is "Yoga for Healthy Ageing," highlighting its role in promoting physical health, mental well-being, emotional resilience and active ageing, contributing to an improved quality of life.

Mr. Modi will address a gathering at the Red Road in central Kolkata while participating in the common yoga protocol session with thousands of yoga practitioners during the programme, an official statement said.

Yoga Day celebrations are being organised across nearly 2,500 locations worldwide, with participation from more than 210 Indian missions and posts, reaffirming yoga’s status as a global movement for health, harmony and collective well-being, it said.

Since its inception in 2015, when the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) adopted India’s proposal to observe June 21 as the International Day of Yoga, the PM has led the celebrations from various locations, including New Delhi, Chandigarh, Lucknow, Mysuru, New York, Srinagar and Vishakhapatnam, the statement added.

Published - June 21, 2026 06:17 am IST

Prime Minister Narendra Modi will on Sunday
(June 21, 2026) morning lead the nation in observing the 12th International Day of Yoga from the Red Road here.
This year's theme for the International Day of Yoga is "Yoga for Healthy Ageing," highlighting its role in promoting physical health, mental well-being, emotional resilience and active ageing, contributing to an improved quality of life.
Mr. Modi will address a gathering at the Red Road in central Kolkata while participating in the common yoga protocol session with thousands of yoga practitioners during the programme, an official statement said.
Yoga Day celebrations are being organised across nearly 2,500 locations worldwide, with participation from more than 210 Indian missions and posts, reaffirming yoga’s status as a global movement for health, harmony and collective well-being, it said.
Since its inception in 2015, when the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) adopted India’s proposal to observe June 21 as the International Day of Yoga, the PM has led the celebrations from various locations, including New Delhi, Chandigarh, Lucknow, Mysuru, New York, Srinagar and Vishakhapatnam, the statement added.
